description: Remove ALL /*turbopackIgnore: true*/ inline comments from the entire codebase and replace them with proper Turbopack configuration in next.config.ts using the native turbopack.ignoreIssue config. This is the proper Turbopack-native way to handle expected dynamic path operations in server modules, instead of littering source code with inline ignore comments.
steps: Step 1 - Remove ALL /*turbopackIgnore: true*/ comments from source files. There are 72 occurrences across these files: src/lib/dataDir.ts, src/lib/tunnel/cloudflared.ts, src/lib/tunnel/tailscaleDaemonRuntime.ts, src/lib/tunnel/tailscaleFunnelRuntime.ts, src/lib/tunnel/tailscaleStatus.ts, src/lib/tunnel/tailscaleInstallRuntime.ts, src/lib/sqliteHelpers.ts, src/lib/localDbStorage.ts, src/lib/sqliteBootstrap.ts, src/mitm/paths.ts, src/shared/services/initializeApp.ts, src/app/api/tunnel/enable/route.ts, src/app/api/tunnel/disable/route.ts, src/app/api/tunnel/tailscale-enable/route.ts, src/app/api/tunnel/tailscale-disable/route.ts. Simply remove the comment text '/*turbopackIgnore: true*/ ' (with trailing space) or '/*turbopackIgnore: true*/' from all path.join, fs.existsSync, import(), and other call sites.

Step 2 - In next.config.ts, add a `turbopack` configuration block with `ignoreIssue` array to suppress NFT warnings for the known server modules that do runtime I/O. Use path patterns matching the tunnel, lib, and mitm modules. Example config:

turbopack: {
  ignoreIssue: [
    { path: /src\/lib\/tunnel\// },
    { path: /src\/lib\/dataDir/ },
    { path: /src\/lib\/sqlite/ },
    { path: /src\/lib\/localDbStorage/ },
    { path: /src\/mitm\/paths/ },
    { path: /src\/shared\/services\/initializeApp/ },
  ]
}

This is the proper native Turbopack configuration for declaring that certain server-only modules are expected to have dynamic path operations.

Step 3 - In the API route files (src/app/api/tunnel/enable/route.ts, disable/route.ts, tailscale-enable/route.ts, tailscale-disable/route.ts), change the dynamic imports from `import(/*turbopackIgnore: true*/ ...)` to plain `import(...)`. These routes use dynamic import() to load runtime modules at request time - which is correct behavior. The turbopack.ignoreIssue config handles the tracing concern.
Step 4 - Verify: run 'source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh && npx tsc --noEmit' passes. Then run 'source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh && npm run build' completes with 0 NFT warnings.
acceptance_criteria: Zero occurrences of 'turbopackIgnore' in any source file under src/
npm run build succeeds with 0 NFT warnings (or if Turbopack still warns despite ignoreIssue, the warnings must be suppressed by the config)
verification: source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh && npm run build 2>&1 | grep -c 'NFT'  (must be 0)
